name: Real Ginger Beef
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Flank steak, sliced as thin as possible across the grain
1 cup Peeled, finely shredded fresh ginger
3 tbsp Shao hsing (rice wine) or domestic dry sherry
2 cups Fresh cilantro leaves, lightly chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the beef in corn starch, soy sauce, sesame oil, and white pepper for 30 minutes, refrigerated.
2. In a separate bowl, toss the ginger with salt and set aside for 20 minutes. Squeeze out as much moisture from the ginger shreds as possible. Set aside.
3. Combine sugar and wine (Shao hsing or domestic dry sherry) in a small dish.
4. Heat oil in a skillet or wok until warm-hot.
5. Add the marinated beef pieces to the hot oil, stirring to separate them. Cook until they change color, then remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Reserve about 3 tablespoons of oil in the pan.
6. In the same pan, add the remaining oil and quickly stir in the ginger for 15 seconds.
7. Stir in the cilantro leaves and wine mixture; cook just until the dish is heated through.
8. Serve immediately.
